title: Do not flatten feature hierarchy into uniform cards
summary: AI-generated feature grids default to a uniform grid of identical icon-headline-body cards, treating every feature as equally important with no visual hierarchy.
instruction: Avoid presenting all features in identical cards. Use varied sizing, staggered layouts, or list formats to signal which features are primary. A flat card grid is acceptable only when all features are genuinely co-equal.
rationale: Uniform feature cards make it impossible for users to distinguish the primary value proposition from secondary features. The pattern hides the product's differentiation behind a wallpaper of equally-weighted claims.
violations: 3x2 or 3x3 grid of cards with identical dimensions
Every card has a colored circle/square icon container of the same size
Every card has a bold short headline with exactly two lines of body copy
Identical border-radius and shadow values on every card
No visual distinction between flagship feature and minor functionality
controls: Staggered card sizes where the primary feature gets more visual weight
Feature list format alternating text and media sections
One primary hero feature above a secondary grid
Card format with intentional variation in media type (icon, screenshot, video)
borderlineCases: Documentation category tiles where features are genuinely co-equal
contextualExceptions: Design system documentation showing all available card variants
detectorSignal: Multiple card elements with identical bounding box dimensions arranged in a regular grid containing icon+headline+body structure.
matcherProposal: Detect repeated card-like containers in grid formation where each contains an icon element, a heading-level text, and a paragraph, with all cards sharing identical CSS dimensions.
detectorLimitations: Cannot distinguish intentional feature parity from template defaulting
falsePositiveScenarios: Product pages where features genuinely are co-equal
mitigationStrategy: Flag with a reminder to review whether the grid represents the actual feature hierarchy or was chosen by default.

fixturePlans.violation: 3x2 grid of identical feature cards with icons in colored circles and two-line descriptions.
fixturePlans.control: Staggered feature layout with one large hero feature card above smaller secondary cards.
fixturePlans.borderline: Co-equal feature grid for a product with equally weighted components.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Not covered by existing rules. Related to layout.centered-everything but focuses on card uniformity within grids.
